sanskrit: यदा हि नेन्द्रियार्थेषु न कर्मस्वनुषज्जते । सर्वसङ्कल्पसंन्यासी योगारूढस्तदोच्यते ॥
hindiTranslation: जिस समय न इन्द्रियोंके भोगोंमें तथा न कर्मोंमें ही आसक्त होता है, उस समय वह सम्पूर्ण संकल्पोंका त्यागी मनुष्य योगारूढ़ कहा जाता है ॥
englishTranslation: When one no longer clings to sense pleasures or to actions, that person is called established in yoga, having renounced all intentions.
essence: Yoga begins when neither pleasure nor action can hook you.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: A person is truly steady in yoga when neither pleasures nor actions hook the mind, and all intentions have been let go.
simpleInsight: There is relief in not being owned by wanting or doing.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Arjuna is frozen, and Krishna is teaching him how to become inwardly steady. After saying that practice leads toward stillness, Krishna now names the state: a person who no longer clings to pleasures or actions is established in yoga.
modernRelevance: You finish the presentation, then keep refreshing the email thread for praise. Or you stop chasing the praise and feel your shoulders drop. This verse names that release.
translationLiteral: यदा (when) हि (indeed) न (not) इन्द्रियार्थेषु (in sense objects) न (not) कर्मसु (in actions) अनुषज्जते (clings) / सर्वसङ्कल्पसंन्यासी (renouncer of all intentions) / योगारूढः (established in yoga) तदा (then) उच्यते (is called)
